oai_response.prompt:
Fix a buggy program written in Python language to solve the following programming problem:
Description: The elections to Berland parliament are happening today. Voting is in full swing!Totally there are n candidates, they are numbered from 1 to n. Based on election results k (1 ≤ k ≤ n) top candidates will take seats in the parliament.After the end of the voting the number of votes for each candidate is calculated. In the resulting table the candidates are ordered by the number of votes. In case of tie (equal number of votes) they are ordered by the time of the last vote given. The candidate with ealier last vote stands higher in the resulting table.So in the resulting table candidates are sorted by the number of votes (more votes stand for the higher place) and if two candidates have equal number of votes they are sorted by the time of last vote (earlier last vote stands for the higher place).There is no way for a candidate with zero votes to take a seat in the parliament. So it is possible that less than k candidates will take a seat in the parliament.In Berland there are m citizens who can vote. Each of them will vote for some candidate. Each citizen will give a vote to exactly one of n candidates. There is no option "against everyone" on the elections. It is not accepted to spoil bulletins or not to go to elections. So each of m citizens will vote for exactly one of n candidates.At the moment a citizens have voted already (1 ≤ a ≤ m). This is an open election, so for each citizen it is known the candidate for which the citizen has voted. Formally, the j-th citizen voted for the candidate gj. The citizens who already voted are numbered in chronological order; i.e. the (j + 1)-th citizen voted after the j-th.The remaining m - a citizens will vote before the end of elections, each of them will vote for one of n candidates.Your task is to determine for each of n candidates one of the three possible outcomes:  a candidate will be elected to the parliament regardless of votes of the remaining m - a citizens;  a candidate has chance to be elected to the parliament after all n citizens have voted;  a candidate has...
Input Specification: The first line contains four integers n, k, m and a (1 ≤ k ≤ n ≤ 100, 1 ≤ m ≤ 100, 1 ≤ a ≤ m) — the number of candidates, the number of seats in the parliament, the number of Berland citizens and the number of citizens who already have voted. The second line contains a sequence of a integers g1, g2, ..., ga (1 ≤ gj ≤ n), where gj is the candidate for which the j-th citizen has voted. Citizens who already voted are numbered in increasing order of voting times.
Output Specification: Print the sequence consisting of n integers r1, r2, ..., rn where:   ri = 1 means that the i-th candidate is guaranteed to take seat in the parliament regardless of votes of the remaining m - a citizens;  ri = 2 means that the i-th candidate has a chance to take a seat in the parliament, i.e. the remaining m - a citizens can vote in such a way that the candidate will take a seat in the parliament;  ri = 3 means that the i-th candidate will not take a seat in the parliament regardless of votes of the remaining m - a citizens. 

Sample Input:
3 1 5 4
1 2 1 3
Sample Output:
1 3 3

Sample Input:
3 1 5 3
1 3 1
Sample Output:
2 3 2

Sample Input:
3 2 5 3
1 3 1
Sample Output:
1 2 2

Notes: None
Take input from standard input and output to standard output

  def __init__(self, cand, votes, last):

    self.votes = votes
    self.last_vote = last

  def beats(self, other, extra):

    votes[cand] += 1

  res = [0 for i in range(candidates)]

  for i in range(candidates):
    if i < candidates - seats:
      low = candidates - seats
      if states[i].beats(states[low], people - voted):
        res[states[i].candidate] = 2
      else:
        res[states[i].candidate] = 3
    else:
      extra = people - voted
      other = i - 1
      place = i

      if extra == 0 and states[i].votes == 0:
        res[states[i].candidate] = 3
        continue

      while other >= 0 and extra > 0:
        needed = states[i].votes - states[other].votes + 1
        if needed <= extra:
          extra -= needed;
          place -= 1
          other -= 1
        else:
          break

      res[states[i].candidate] = (1 if place + seats >= candidates else 2)

  for i in res:
    print(i, end = ' ')

main()

message.content:
```python
class State:
    __slots__ = ['candidate', 'votes', 'last_vote']

    def __init__(self, cand, votes, last):
        self.candidate = cand
        self.votes = votes
        self.last_vote = last

    def beats(self, other, extra):
        if self.votes + extra > other.votes:
            return True
        elif self.votes + extra == other.votes:
            return self.last_vote < other.last_vote
        else:
            return False

def main():
    candidates, seats, people, voted = map(int, input().split())
    votes = [0 for i in range(candidates)]
    last_vote = [0 for i in range(candidates)]

    if candidates == 1:
        print(1)
        return

    v = list(map(int, input().split()))
    for t in range(voted):
        cand = v[t] - 1
        votes[cand] += 1
        last_vote[cand] = t

    states = [State(i, votes[i], last_vote[i]) for i in range(candidates)]
    states_sorted = sorted(states, key=lambda x: (-x.votes, x.last_vote))
    res = [0 for i in range(candidates)]

    for i in range(candidates):
        current = states[i]
        # Check if guaranteed to be in parliament (1)
        # Scenario 1: Already in top k and cannot be pushed out
        # Scenario 2: Can be in top k even after remaining votes
        # Otherwise, check if possible to be in top k (2)
        # Else, 3

        # First, check if current is in top k with current votes
        # We need to see if it's possible for others to overtake current
        # Also, check if current can stay in top k even with remaining votes

        # To determine if current is guaranteed (1), we need to see if even in worst case (others get all remaining votes), current stays in top k
        # To determine if current is possible (2), we need to see if in best case (current gets all remaining votes), current can be in top k

        # First, check if current is in top k with current votes
        temp_states = states.copy()
        temp_states_sorted = sorted(temp_states, key=lambda x: (-x.votes, x.last_vote))
        current_rank = -1
        for idx, state in enumerate(temp_states_sorted):
            if state.candidate == current.candidate:
                current_rank = idx
                break

        if current_rank < seats and current.votes > 0:
            # Now, check if it's possible for others to push current out
            # Worst case: all remaining votes go to the candidate just below current in the ranking
            remaining_votes = people - voted
            if remaining_votes == 0:
                res[current.candidate] = 1
                continue

            # Find the candidate who is at position 'seats' (0-based)
            if seats < candidates:
                threshold_candidate = temp_states_sorted[seats]
                # Check if current can be beaten by threshold_candidate with remaining votes
                if threshold_candidate.beats(current, remaining_votes):
                    res[current.candidate] = 2
                else:
                    res[current.candidate] = 1
            else:
                res[current.candidate] = 1
        else:
            # Current is not in top k with current votes. Check if it's possible to get into top k with remaining votes
            if current.votes + (people - voted) > 0:
                # Check if it's possible to get into top k
                # Best case: all remaining votes go to current
                best_case_votes = current.votes + (people - voted)
                # Now, compare with the k-th candidate
                if seats > 0:
                    kth_candidate = temp_states_sorted[seats - 1]
                    if best_case_votes > kth_candidate.votes or (best_case_votes == kth_candidate.votes and current.last_vote < kth_candidate.last_vote):
                        res[current.candidate] = 2
                    else:
                        res[current.candidate] = 3
                else:
                    res[current.candidate] = 3
            else:
                res[current.candidate] = 3

    print(' '.join(map(str, res)))

main()
```

```python
class State:
    __slots__ = ['candidate', 'votes', 'last_vote']

    def __init__(self, cand, votes, last):
        self.candidate = cand
        self.votes = votes
        self.last_vote = last

    def beats(self, other, extra):
        return self.votes + extra > other.votes or (self.votes + extra == other.votes and self.last_vote < other.last_vote)

def main():
    candidates, seats, people, voted = map(int, input().split())
    votes = [0 for i in range(candidates)]
    last_vote = [0 for i in range(candidates)]

    if candidates == 1:
        print(1)
        return

    v = list(map(int, input().split()))
    for t in range(voted):
        cand = v[t] - 1
        votes[cand] += 1
        last_vote[cand] = t

    states = [State(i, votes[i], last_vote[i]) for i in range(candidates)]
    states_sorted = sorted(states, key=lambda x: (-x.votes, x.last_vote))
    res = [0 for i in range(candidates)]

    for i in range(candidates):
        current = states[i]
        # Check if the candidate can be guaranteed a seat (1)
        # Scenario 1: The candidate is in top k and cannot be pushed out by remaining votes
        # Scenario 2: The candidate is not in top k but can be pushed in with remaining votes
        # Scenario 3: The candidate cannot be in top k regardless of remaining votes

        # First, check if the candidate is in the top k with current votes
        # We need to consider the worst case for the candidate (others get all remaining votes)
        # Also, we need to consider the best case for the candidate (they get all remaining votes)

        # Best case: current gets all remaining votes
        best_votes = current.votes + (people - voted)
        best_last = current.last_vote if (people - voted) == 0 else voted + (people - voted) - 1

        # Worst case: others get all remaining votes
        worst_votes = current.votes
        worst_last = current.last_vote

        # Create a list of all candidates' votes in the worst case (others get all remaining votes)
        temp_states = []
        for j in range(candidates):
            if j == i:
                temp_states.append(State(j, worst_votes, worst_last))
            else:
                temp_states.append(State(j, states[j].votes + (people - voted), voted + (people - voted) - 1))

        # Sort the temp_states to see if the candidate is still in top k
        temp_sorted = sorted(temp_states, key=lambda x: (-x.votes, x.last_vote))
        in_top_k_worst = False
        for pos in range(seats):
            if pos >= len(temp_sorted):
                break
            if temp_sorted[pos].candidate == i:
                in_top_k_worst = True
                break

        if in_top_k_worst:
            res[i] = 1
            continue

        # Check if the candidate can be in top k in the best case
        temp_states_best = []
        for j in range(candidates):
            if j == i:
                temp_states_best.append(State(j, best_votes, best_last))
            else:
                temp_states_best.append(State(j, states[j].votes, states[j].last_vote))

        temp_sorted_best = sorted(temp_states_best, key=lambda x: (-x.votes, x.last_vote))
        in_top_k_best = False
        for pos in range(seats):
            if pos >= len(temp_sorted_best):
                break
            if temp_sorted_best[pos].candidate == i:
                in_top_k_best = True
                break

        if in_top_k_best:
            res[i] = 2
        else:
            res[i] = 3

    print(' '.join(map(str, res)))

main()
```

```python
class State:
    __slots__ = ['candidate', 'votes', 'last_vote']

    def __init__(self, cand, votes, last):
        self.candidate = cand
        self.votes = votes
        self.last_vote = last

    def beats(self, other, extra):
        if self.votes + extra > other.votes:
            return True
        elif self.votes + extra == other.votes:
            return self.last_vote < other.last_vote
        else:
            return False

def main():
    candidates, seats, people, voted = map(int, input().split())
    votes = [0 for i in range(candidates)]
    last_vote = [0 for i in range(candidates)]

    if candidates == 1:
        print(1)
        return

    v = list(map(int, input().split()))
    for t in range(voted):
        cand = v[t] - 1
        votes[cand] += 1
        last_vote[cand] = t

    states = [State(i, votes[i], last_vote[i]) for i in range(candidates)]
    states_sorted = sorted(states, key=lambda x: (-x.votes, x.last_vote))
    res = [0 for i in range(candidates)]

    for i in range(candidates):
        current = states[i]
        # Check if the candidate can be guaranteed a seat (1)
        # Scenario 1: Already in top k and cannot be pushed out
        # Scenario 2: Can be pushed out by others
        # Check if the candidate is in top k after all votes
        # To be guaranteed, even if all remaining votes go to others, still in top k
        # To have a chance, there exists a way to get into top k
        # Otherwise, 3

        # First, check if the candidate is already in top k and cannot be pushed out
        # To check this, see if even if all remaining votes go to the next candidates, the current candidate stays in top k
        # Also, if the candidate has 0 votes and remaining votes are 0, they cannot be in top k
        if current.votes == 0 and people - voted == 0:
            res[current.candidate] = 3
            continue

        # Check if the candidate is in the top k in the current state
        # Sort the states by votes and last_vote
        temp_states = [State(s.candidate, s.votes, s.last_vote) for s in states]
        temp_states_sorted = sorted(temp_states, key=lambda x: (-x.votes, x.last_vote))
        current_rank = -1
        for idx in range(len(temp_states_sorted)):
            if temp_states_sorted[idx].candidate == current.candidate:
                current_rank = idx
                break

        if current_rank < seats:
            # Now, check if the candidate can be pushed out
            # The worst case is when all remaining votes go to the candidates just below
            remaining_votes = people - voted
            can_push_out = False
            if seats < candidates:
                # The candidate at position seats is the one to beat
                threshold = temp_states_sorted[seats - 1]
                # To push out current, the threshold needs to have votes > current or same votes and earlier last vote
                # So, check if the threshold can be overtaken by others
                # The worst case for current is when remaining votes go to the threshold candidate
                # So, check if current can still stay above threshold in this case
                if threshold.votes + remaining_votes > current.votes:
                    can_push_out = True
                elif threshold.votes + remaining_votes == current.votes and threshold.last_vote < current.last_vote:
                    can_push_out = True
                else:
                    can_push_out = False
            else:
                can_push_out = False

            if not can_push_out:
                res[current.candidate] = 1
            else:
                res[current.candidate] = 2
        else:
            # The candidate is not in top k currently. Can they get into top k?
            # The best case is when all remaining votes go to this candidate
            new_votes = current.votes + (people - voted)
            if new_votes == 0:
                res[current.candidate] = 3
                continue

            # Now, compare with the current k-th candidate
            # The current k-th candidate is temp_states_sorted[seats - 1]
            threshold = temp_states_sorted[seats - 1]
            if new_votes > threshold.votes:
                res[current.candidate] = 2
            elif new_votes == threshold.votes and current.last_vote < threshold.last_vote:
                res[current.candidate] = 2
            else:
                # Also, check if there's a candidate with 0 votes in top k and remaining votes can push them out
                # If the threshold has 0 votes and remaining votes are >0, then current can get in
                if threshold.votes == 0 and (people - voted) > 0:
                    res[current.candidate] = 2
                else:
                    res[current.candidate] = 3

    print(' '.join(map(str, res)))

main()
```

source_data.prob_desc_input_spec: The first line contains four integers n, k, m and a (1 ≤ k ≤ n ≤ 100, 1 ≤ m ≤ 100, 1 ≤ a ≤ m) — the number of candidates, the number of seats in the parliament, the number of Berland citizens and the number of citizens who already have voted. The second line contains a sequence of a integers g1, g2, ..., ga (1 ≤ gj ≤ n), where gj is the candidate for which the j-th citizen has voted. Citizens who already voted are numbered in increasing order of voting times.
source_data.prob_desc_output_spec: Print the sequence consisting of n integers r1, r2, ..., rn where:   ri = 1 means that the i-th candidate is guaranteed to take seat in the parliament regardless of votes of the remaining m - a citizens;  ri = 2 means that the i-th candidate has a chance to take a seat in the parliament, i.e. the remaining m - a citizens can vote in such a way that the candidate will take a seat in the parliament;  ri = 3 means that the i-th candidate will not take a seat in the parliament regardless of votes of the remaining m - a citizens.
source_data.prob_desc_description: The elections to Berland parliament are happening today. Voting is in full swing!Totally there are n candidates, they are numbered from 1 to n. Based on election results k (1 ≤ k ≤ n) top candidates will take seats in the parliament.After the end of the voting the number of votes for each candidate is calculated. In the resulting table the candidates are ordered by the number of votes. In case of tie (equal number of votes) they are ordered by the time of the last vote given. The candidate with ealier last vote stands higher in the resulting table.So in the resulting table candidates are sorted by the number of votes (more votes stand for the higher place) and if two candidates have equal number of votes they are sorted by the time of last vote (earlier last vote stands for the higher place).There is no way for a candidate with zero votes to take a seat in the parliament. So it is possible that less than k candidates will take a seat in the parliament.In Berland there are m citizens who can vote. Each of them will vote for some candidate. Each citizen will give a vote to exactly one of n candidates. There is no option "against everyone" on the elections. It is not accepted to spoil bulletins or not to go to elections. So each of m citizens will vote for exactly one of n candidates.At the moment a citizens have voted already (1 ≤ a ≤ m). This is an open election, so for each citizen it is known the candidate for which the citizen has voted. Formally, the j-th citizen voted for the candidate gj. The citizens who already voted are numbered in chronological order; i.e. the (j + 1)-th citizen voted after the j-th.The remaining m - a citizens will vote before the end of elections, each of them will vote for one of n candidates.Your task is to determine for each of n candidates one of the three possible outcomes:  a candidate will be elected to the parliament regardless of votes of the remaining m - a citizens;  a candidate has chance to be elected to the parliament after all n citizens have voted;  a candidate has no chances to be elected to the parliament regardless of votes of the remaining m - a citizens.
